channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's Oval Office thrashing of Zelenskyy shows limits of Western allies' ability to sway US leader
item.description: President Donald Trump’s Oval Office thrashing of Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy has laid bare the limits of a full-court press by America’s allies aimed at reshaping Trump’s determination to end Russia’s invasion even if the terms are not to Ukraine’s liking.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump staffers load boxes of items seized by FBI in 2022 Mar-a-Lago raid onto Air Force One
item.description: President Trump's staff loaded boxes of items taken from his home in Florida during an FBI raid in 2022 onto Air Force One at Joint Base Andrew Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to sign order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: The first time Donald Trump spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, he tried to pressure Ukraine’s new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: European leaders pledge to stand by Ukraine after confrontational Oval Office meeting with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump's order making English the official language in the US could mean
item.description: As President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States, activists and advocacy groups are alarmed by what that will mean for non-English speakers when it comes to immigration, voter access and other issues.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's top prosecutor in DC demotes several supervisors who handled politically sensitive cases
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Several senior leaders in Washington's federal prosecutors office have been demoted to jobs handling misdemeanors or other low-level matters, in the latest move by President Donald Trump's U.S. attorney to roil the office that oversaw the massive prosecution of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and BYRON TAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy can 'come back when he is ready for Peace' after fiery White House exchange
item.description: An effort to advance a peace deal between Ukraine and Russia came to a halt Friday after President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy sparred in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Those with gender dysphoria can no longer serve in military: Pentagon
item.description: (NewsNation) — A memo sent to Defense Department leaders Thursday states those with gender dysphoria are no longer eligible for military service. This document, written by Darin S.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Republican senators threaten not to boost Texas public universities’ funding over DEI ban
item.description: In a letter, Sens. Brandon Creighton and Paul Bettencourt said they found “numerous” violations of the law, but didn’t provide specifics.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump calls for ‘ceasefire now’ between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump says he wants an “immediate” ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine and has warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to make peace or lose American support.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 1 thing that upended years of U.S. foreign policy
item.description: Three years after Russia launched its invasion of Ukraine, the U.S., in a split with its European allies over the war, sided with Russia at the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: President Donald Trump berated Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved that country closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social Security Administration could cut up to 50% of its workforce
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is preparing to lay off at least 7,000 people from its workforce of 60,000, according to a person familiar with the agency’s plans who is not authorized to speak publicly.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prince Harry's immigration status under fire over drugs, possible lies
item.description: Prince Harry's immigration status has come under fire after he admitted to recreational use of marijuana, cocaine and psychedelics.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats push for more ballot drop boxes to avoid election delays
item.description: Nevada Democrats unveiled a proposal Thursday to increase the number of ballot drop boxes in the days before an election.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Red Wing Public Schools cancel event with Attorney General Keith Ellison as speaker
item.description: Superintendent Bob Jaszczak wrote in an email to parents the event was canceled over concerns of “significant disruption,” but didn’t specify what those disruptions could be.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Giving US ownership of Ukraine's minerals would help keep peace: Hassett
item.description: Kevin Hassett said in an interview with NewsNation's Kellie Meyer that these minerals' estimated worth is $20B.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada: Here's when
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting next Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How Trump’s crackdown plays into misperceptions about immigrants and crime
item.description: The White House portrays its immigration crackdown as a success. Critics say the administration is trying to look tough for the cameras, and worry about the “tail wagging the dog.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: DFL, GOP team up on bills to battle absenteeism in Minnesota schools
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers this week are moving forward on five bills aimed at addressing chronic absenteeism in Minnesota schools, and DFLers and Republicans are working together on policy solutions.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Federal freeze on energy and climate funding causing concern, confusion in Minnesota
item.description: Some of the money has been released since federal judges ordered the funding to be unfrozen. But weatherization programs and rebates for home efficiency upgrades are still on hold. State and local officials say a lack of communication has led to confusion and uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News
